zh.lifeSummary: Richard Preston是John Preston的兒子，於1454年出生在英國戴文斯塔福頓。他於大約1479年與Agnes Harrington在英國戴文斯塔福頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Preston是John Preston的儿子，于1454年出生在英国戴文斯塔福頓。他于大约1479年与Agnes Harrington在英国戴文斯塔福頓结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Preston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from one of the many places (most notably one in Lancashire) so called from Old English prēost ‘priest’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.
occasionally from Middle English prest + son ‘son of the priest’. Priests were forbidden marriage but many produced illegitimate offspring.

variants.name: Presson
meanings: English (Cheshire and Lancashire): variant of   Preston  .
French: occupational name for a presser of wine or oil, from a derivative of presser ‘to press’.

variants.name: Prestwich
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from a place in Lancashire (now Greater Manchester), so called from Old English prēost ‘priest’ + wīc ‘outlying settlement’, or from other places with the same derivation. Variants such as Prestidge, Prestage, etc., parallel the local pronunciation of the Lancashire name. Compare Preston .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Preston was born in 1454, in Staverton, Devon, England as the son of John Preston. He married Agnes Harrington about 1479, in Staverton, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Preston est né en 1454, à Staverton, Devon, Angleterre, fils de John Preston. Il a épousé Agnes Harrington environ 1479, à Staverton, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Preston wurde 1454 in Staverton, Devon, England, als Sohn von John Preston geboren. Er hat um 1479 in Staverton, Devon, England Agnes Harrington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Preston è nato il nel 1454 nel Staverton, Devon, England come figlio di John Preston. Lui sposato Agnes Harrington circa 1479, nel Staverton, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Staverton, Devon, England: イギリスデボンスタバートン
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Prestonは1454年にイギリスデボンスタバートンでJohn Prestonの息子として生まれた。約1479年にイギリスデボンスタバートンでAgnes Harringtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Preston(은)는 1454년 영국 데폰 스타버턴에서 John Preston의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1479년 영국 데폰 스타버턴에서 Agnes Harrington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Preston nasceu em 1454, em Staverton, Devon, Inglaterra filho de John Preston. Ele casou-se com Agnes Harrington aproximadamente 1479, em Staverton, Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Preston родился в 1454 г. в местности ставиртон, девон, Англия. Его отца звали John Preston. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Harrington около 1479 г. в местности ставиртон, девон, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Preston nació en 1454, en Staverton, Devon, Inglaterra como hijo de John Preston. Se casó con Agnes Harrington aproximadamente 1479, en Staverton, Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
